title: MyHC-IIb/Laminin Fresh Frozen
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b4dra","text":"Myosin heavy chain IIb/laminin immunofluorescence staining protocol for fresh frozen skeletal muscle.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw sections, dry for ~30 mins, pap, and label. The MyHC-IIb will, under no circumstances, work with fixed tissue. Heat-induced anti retrieval has been reported to work: Esper ME, Kodippili K, Rudnicki MA.. Immunofluorescence Labeling of Skeletal Muscle in Development, Regeneration, and Disease. Methods in Molecular Biology. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-1-0716-2675-7_9 however, we have not been able to get this to work ourselves.
Block M.O.M.® (Mouse on Mouse) Blocking Reagent Vector Laboratories Catalog #MKB-2213-1 01:00:00 Room temperature

Incubate primaries in 1% BSA: BF-F3 (MHC IIb) Developmental Studies Hybridoma Bank Catalog #BF-F3 1:10 Anti-Laminin antibody produced in rabbit Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L9393 (RRID: AB_477163) 1:1000 Overnight 4 °C

Incubate secondaries in 1% BSA: Alexa Fluor® 647 AffiniPure™ Goat Anti-Mouse IgM, µ chain specific Jackson ImmunoResearch Laboratories, Inc. Catalog #115-605-075 (RRID: AB_2338911) 1:1000 Alexa Fluor® 488 AffiniPure™ Goat Anti-Rabbit IgG (H L) Jackson ImmunoResearch Laboratories, Inc. Catalog #111-545-003 (RRID: AB_2338046) 1:1000 DAPI Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #D9542 1 μg/ml 02:00:00 Room temperature
Mount with aqueous mounting media. Image within 2 days.
creator: Griffen Wakelin
